title: Friesodielsia calycina
uses: Friesodielsia calycina has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian folk medicine, particularly in Malaysia and Indonesia, for its potential medicinal properties. The plant has been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. In traditional medicine, Friesodielsia calycina is used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin infections. Its leaves are often used to make tea or infusions, which are believed to have therapeutic effects. However, further scientific research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.
taxonomy: Friesodielsia calycina is a plant species within the family Annonaceae. It belongs to the genus Friesodielsia. The classification includes the kingdom Plantae, division Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. Friesodielsia calycina is further classified into the order Magnoliales and family Annonaceae. Within the genus, it is categorized as a member of the Friesodielsia genus.
